name: Manage Helm Releases
description: Upgrade, rollback, and manage the lifecycle of Helm releases with revision history, atomic upgrades, and cleanup strategies.
prerequisites: An existing Helm release in a cluster

steps.title: Check current release status
steps.description: View the current state and revision of a release.

steps.title: Upgrade a release with new values
steps.description: Apply new configuration to an existing release.
steps.command: helm upgrade my-release bitnami/nginx --namespace production --set replicaCount=3 --atomic --timeout 5m
steps.tip: The --atomic flag automatically rolls back the upgrade if it fails, preventing broken deployments.

steps.title: View release history
steps.description: List all revisions of a release for auditing and rollback reference.

steps.title: Rollback to a previous revision
steps.description: Revert a release to a known-good state.
steps.warning: Rollback creates a new revision, it does not delete history. Verify the target revision number with 'helm history' first.

steps.title: Uninstall a release
steps.description: Remove a release and its associated resources from the cluster.
steps.tip: Use --keep-history to preserve release records for auditing. Without it, all history is deleted.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Helm Release Management Workflow
set -euo pipefail

RELEASE=${1:?"Usage: $0 <release-name> <namespace>"}
NAMESPACE=${2:-"default"}

echo "=== Current Release Status ==="
helm status "$RELEASE" -n "$NAMESPACE" 2>/dev/null || echo "Release not found"

echo ""
echo "=== Release History ==="
helm history "$RELEASE" -n "$NAMESPACE" --max 10 2>/dev/null || echo "No history available"

echo ""
echo "=== Current Values ==="
helm get values "$RELEASE" -n "$NAMESPACE" 2>/dev/null || echo "No values found"

echo ""
echo "=== Manifests Diff (if upgrading) ==="
echo "Run: helm diff upgrade $RELEASE <chart> -n $NAMESPACE -f values.yaml"

echo ""
echo "=== Quick Commands ==="
echo "Upgrade:  helm upgrade $RELEASE <chart> -n $NAMESPACE --atomic --timeout 5m"
echo "Rollback: helm rollback $RELEASE <revision> -n $NAMESPACE --wait"
echo "Delete:   helm uninstall $RELEASE -n $NAMESPACE --keep-history"

faq.question: What does --atomic do during an upgrade?
faq.answer: The --atomic flag rolls back the upgrade automatically if any resource fails to become ready within the timeout period. It combines --wait with automatic rollback on failure.

faq.question: How many revisions does Helm keep by default?
faq.answer: Helm keeps a maximum of 10 revisions by default. You can change this with --history-max on install or upgrade. Old revisions are automatically pruned when the limit is exceeded.
